steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage notes that critics drew similarities between film and traditional art to help legitimize film, showing they wanted to shape how the public sees cinema.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content:
D perception of means the way the public sees or understands cinema, which matches the idea that critics’ comparisons help shape that view to legitimize film.
The other choices either describe actions the public does (reproduction, contribution, application) rather than how the public sees cinema or do not convey the needed sense of influence on opinion.
That confirms D is correct.
